title: Reduce IP registration burn fee to 0.02 IPGT and persist Internet Identity login sessions

requirements.text: In the backend (backend/main.mo), reduce the IP registration burn fee constant from 100 IPGT to 0.02 IPGT. Given that IPGT uses 2 decimal places of precision (base units), this means the burn amount must be set to 2 base units (i.e., 1/50th of 1 IPGT coin). Update all relevant constants, minimum balance checks, and burn logic accordingly.

quotes: the MIN burn fee for an IP is 1/50 of a IPGT Coin please adjust
Burn fee reduced to 0.02 IPGT (1/50th of 1 IPGT coin)

acceptanceCriteria: The backend burn constant is set to 2 base units (0.02 IPGT at 2 decimal precision)
Registering an IP deducts exactly 0.02 IPGT from the caller's balance
The minimum balance check for registration is updated to require at least 0.02 IPGT
Total burned supply is incremented by 0.02 IPGT per successful registration

requirements.text: Update the IP Registration page (frontend/src/pages/RegisterIP.tsx) and the RegistrationSuccessModal (frontend/src/components/RegistrationSuccessModal.tsx) to display the correct burn fee of 0.02 IPGT instead of 100 IPGT. Any UI text, tooltips, or helper copy referencing the burn amount must reflect the new fee.

quotes: Registration burns 100 IPGT from your balance permanently
update the frontend registration UI text so it accurately reflects the new fee

acceptanceCriteria: The registration form displays '0.02 IPGT' as the burn fee
The success modal shows '0.02 IPGT burned' after a successful registration
No UI text anywhere in the app references 100 IPGT as the registration cost

requirements.text: Ensure the Internet Identity authentication hook (frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts) persists the authenticated session across page reloads using AuthClient's local storage persistence. On app startup, the app must automatically restore an existing authenticated session without requiring the user to log in again. The session should remain valid until the user explicitly logs out or the session naturally expires.

quotes: save any log in created so they can log in in the future and keep their data
Persistent login sessions — the Internet Identity session is saved in the browser so users don't have to log in every time they revisit the app
Session restoration — on page load, the app will automatically check for an existing Internet Identity session and restore the logged-in state without requiring re-authentication

acceptanceCriteria: After logging in with Internet Identity, refreshing the page restores the authenticated state automatically without showing the login prompt
The user's principal ID and IPGT balance are displayed immediately on page load if a valid session exists
Clicking logout clears the session and the user is returned to the unauthenticated state
If no valid session exists on load, the app shows the unauthenticated state without errors

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts or frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts directly if they are immutable — implement session persistence using only patterns supported by the existing AuthClient setup in those hooks
Do not change the IPGT token supply or any other token parameters beyond the burn fee constant
All on-chain data (IP records, balances) must remain linked to user principals and must not be reset
nonGoals: Adding new authentication providers beyond Internet Identity
Changing any other backend token parameters (supply, transfer logic, etc.)
Redesigning any UI pages beyond updating the burn fee display text
Implementing a coin faucet or airdrop mechanism
